title: pgx Pooling, Query Contracts, Transactions, and Cancellation · Language Lens: Feed Go Transaction Connection
summary: Handlers create pools, query rows are not closed, transactions use the wrong connection, contexts are replaced, SQL errors lose identity, and shutdown races active work. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go transaction connection increment in bounded pgx repository and unit-of-work layer. Learners progress through prediction, expli…
objectives: Run every unit-of-work statement on one transaction, defer rollback safely, commit once, and preserve the original cause.
Use parameterized typed queries, explicit columns, row closure, iteration errors, cardinality expectations, and explainable mapping.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Transaction Connection · Theory Feed Go Transaction Connect…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 9038 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove pgx pool, rows, one-connection transactions, cancellation, and error ownership; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Run every unit-of-work statement on one transaction, defer rollback safely, commit once, and preserve the original cause.
